narrative_summary:
The BRCA2 c.6412G>T (p.Val2138Phe, V2138F) missense change occurs in the DNA‑repair gene BRCA2 and has been evaluated in the context of hereditary breast and ovarian cancer risk.  A prevalence study of a Brazilian at‑risk cohort reported the variant as a variant of uncertain significance (VUS) without a definitive pathogenic or benign classification [PMC5348334].  A Mexican screening effort likewise identified V2138F as a VUS, noting a benign in‑silico prediction but retaining the uncertain clinical label [PMC3360054].  An analysis using hierarchical modeling of rare BRCA1/2 missense variants included V2138F among the variants examined but did not assign a specific risk estimate, indicating only that it co‑occurred with other variants in the regression model [PMC3111904].  In contrast, a 2022 study of solid‑tumor cytologic cell blocks listed V2138F as one of the pathogenic BRCA2 alterations detected in that cohort [PMC9972011].

No experimental functional data for V2138F were presented in the extracted literature.

Population data from a South African next‑generation sequencing survey showed the variant at very low allele frequencies across African, European and South Asian ancestries, but did not provide an interpretation of clinical impact [PMC7908826].  Computational assessment in a Brazilian patient series classified V2138F as benign based on multiple prediction tools (PolyPhen‑2, SIFT, Align‑GVGD) and recorded it as benign in the IARC‑LOVD database [PMC4038072].

Overall, the variant is most frequently reported as a VUS in hereditary cancer screening studies, while in silico analyses consistently predict a benign effect and a single tumor‑sequencing report describes it as pathogenic.  In the absence of functional validation, the balance of evidence leans toward a likely benign or low‑risk interpretation, but the variant remains classified as uncertain in the context of hereditary breast‑ovarian cancer risk.

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The study reports the BRCA2 V2138F variant as a variant of uncertain significance with no clear pathogenic or benign classification, and provides limited family segregation data without interpretive conclusions.
article_mentions.title: Prevalence of BRCA1/BRCA2 mutations in a Brazilian population sample at-risk for hereditary breast cancer and characterization of its genetic ancestry

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The study surveys BRCA2 founder variants in South Africa and reports that the V2138F (c.6412G>T) variant is present at low frequencies in multiple ancestries, but does not provide functional or clinical interpretation of its significance.
article_mentions.title: Globally Rare BRCA2 Variants With Founder Haplotypes in the South African Population: Implications for Point-of-Care Testing Based on a Single-Institution BRCA1/2 Next-Generation Sequencing Study

mentions.quoted_text: The parameters of the model were estimated using a method that involves pseudo likelihood estimation of the relative risk parameters with Bayesian estimation of the variance components, a strategy that has been shown to have the best statistical properties among competitor estimation techniques studied in this setting [Capanu and Begg, 2010]. This approach approximates the mixed logistic regression model with a linear mixed model using the SAS %glimmix macro. The SAS Proc Mixed with the Prior option is employed to estimate the variance of the random effects using a random walk Metropolis-Hastings algorithm with a non-informative Inverse-Gamma distribution. Once the Bayesian estimate of the variance is obtained, the %glimmix macro is employed again to estimate the regression parameters using the pseudo-likelihood method but with the random effects' variance pre-specified at the Bayesian estimate. For purposes of benchmarking, we also employed ordinary logistic regression including in the model all variants that were observed in at least one case and one control and adjusting for the same confounders as the first stage model of the hierarchical model. We note that two variants (**V2138F** and F486L) co-occurred with other variants and were included on their own in a logistic regression model adjusted for the same confounders as described above. We also compared these results with estimates obtained from a hierarchical model in which the second-stage model included no covariates besides an intercept (i.e. assuming exchangeability of the random effects).

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The study applies hierarchical modeling to assess rare BRCA1/2 missense variants, highlighting a subset with adverse bioinformatic scores as potentially deleterious, but does not provide a specific risk assessment for the V2138F variant.
article_mentions.title: Assessment of Rare BRCA1 and BRCA2 Variants of Unknown Significance Using Hierarchical Modeling

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The study screened BRCA1/2 in Mexican women, identifying several pathogenic mutations and reporting BRCA2 p.V2138F as a variant of uncertain significance with a benign in‑silico prediction.
article_mentions.title: Full-Exon Pyrosequencing Screening of BRCA Germline Mutations in Mexican Women with Inherited Breast and Ovarian Cancer

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The article reports that BRCA2 p.V2138F was detected as one of the pathogenic variants in cytologic cell block samples sequenced for solid tumors, highlighting its presence in the cohort studied.
article_mentions.title: Utilization of cytologic cell blocks for targeted sequencing of solid tumors
